text: Yes - unsupervised Leiden clustering of the dependency graph recovers Mathlib's named domains without labels: 138 clusters over ~92,594 theorems, 121 pure (one dominant module) and 17 mixed, concurring with prior Louvain results (Li et al. 2026, NMI=0.34). The domain structure of mathematics is latent in proof dependencies.

comments.caveat: TENSION (e-0062/a-0060, q-0022): node-level Leiden-vs-module agreement is only NMI 0.30 / ARI 0.14, with modules spread across ~22 clusters each (median 44% in largest). 'Recovers named domains' should be read as 'clusters are NAMEABLE by a plurality module (~30% dominance)', not as faithful reproduction of the module partition.
